content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled data gets stored and consolidated into an active learning agent's long-term memory, creating a self-sustaining 'curiosity filter' that outperforms current uncertainty-based SOTA active learning memory pipelines from 2024-2025. This filter automatically retains high-potential, information-rich data that aligns with the agent's open-ended learning goals without manual tuning of selection criteria.
